channel.title: Engineering Management Institute
channel.description: The Engineering Management Institute can help you to Attract, Develop, & Retain Top Engineering Talent

item.title: About
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
The ability of engineers and engineering organizations to be successful is heavily reliant on their ability to manage effectively. Their success relies on their ability to manage people, projects, budgets, schedules, and risk.  Whether or not they can communicate effectively, present ideas powerfully, build relationships, remain productive, and lead, matters.

Engineering projects don’t just happen because engineers are technically proficient.  They happen because...

  • An engineer who was able to speak effectively was able to sell the project to a municipality or a private client, or
  • an engineer who could communicate well saved the project in its early stages by working with several parties to overcome challenges, or
  • an engineer effectively led a team to turn an idea into reality.

These are all engineering management skills, but they are harder to come by than you may think.

 

Anthony Fasano, PE, founder of The Engineering Management Institute (EMI) learned this the hard way.  After a turbulent start to his civil engineering career, he left the industry, dejected and depressed.  However his love for the profession quickly brought him back and the first thing he did was interview successful engineering professionals.  These interviews made something very clear: successful engineers invested heavily in their management skills and developed their leadership abilities.

Fasano immediately started building these skills and his career took off.  He rapidly advanced in a highly reputable civil engineering firm.  As happy as Fasano was to advance, he felt a desire to help other engineers develop these management skills, and therefore he took massive action.  He enrolled in an top executive coaching school and began public speaking training and set out to help as many engineers as possible.

Since then, Fasano has written the bestselling book Engineer Your Own Success (which was picked up by IEEE-Wiley Press), spoken to thousands of engineers across the country, and built the Engineering Management Institute - a company focused on helping engineers become effective managers and leaders through powerful content and corporate training.  The website contains free articles, two podcasts (The Engineering Career Coach Podcast and The Civil Engineering Podcast) and a YouTube series, Engineering Career TV.  The TECC podcast was recently cited by Forbes as one of the 15 most inspiring podcasts for professionals.

Fasano founded The Engineering Management Institute to bring his passion for engineering management training to engineering organizations worldwide through customized on site and online training.  Whether you are a small business, large corporation or nonprofit association, EMI can help your employees become effective managers and leaders.

Contact us here for more information on how we can help you.

- - - - - - - - - - - - -

A message from our founder…

When I first decided to try to help engineers develop their management skills on a broad scale, people told me I wouldn’t succeed.  They said engineers aren’t interested in developing these skills and they certainly wouldn’t listen to a podcast on this topic.  Well, 2.5 million downloads later and a Forbes citation has proven them wrong.

Engineers with management skills can accomplish anything, for themselves and their firms, and our mission is to make sure that sustainable engineering management learning opportunities are available to every organization and engineer that needs them.

Let us help you succeed.

To your success,

Anthony Fasano, PE Author of Engineer Your Own Success President & CEO of the Engineering Management Institute

item.title: What is CMAP Assessment
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
The CMAP Assessment Case Study and the
4 Key Drivers of Successful Engineering Managers...

What if there was an assessment for engineering professionals, different than the DISC or Meyers Briggs? One that could assess the current management abilities of your engineering professionals, to provide them (and you) with valuable information on how and where to improve.

Through our relationships with many engineering firms, we’ve recognized that it is nearly impossible for firms to accurately identify which engineers need management training, and on what skillsets. Therefore, many firms are spending money on training without being sure that it is needed, or not focusing their resources on the right people or departments.

At the Engineering Management Institute (EMI), we have a goal of solving this problem, and we’d like you to be a part of the solution. We’ve been working on an assessment tool that can determine an engineering professional’s current abilities to be an effective and profitable manager, based on success markers of over one thousand past and current successful engineering managers.

And now, we are in the final stages of completing this assessment tool, and we’d like to invite your firm to be part of this exciting process!

Introducing the CMAP Assessment Tool…

The Current Management Abilities Potential Assessment (CMAP) was built with the help of an outside data analytics company, and a consortium of leading engineering firms across the country.

The data used to build the assessment tool was and will be taken from three sources including our hundreds of podcast interviews with successful engineering professionals, a survey of our 15,000-person email list, and a case study that will include a survey of at least 200 current engineering managers, their reports and their managers on their effectiveness as managers.

To properly develop this assessment tool, we are looking for firms to be part of the final case study for the CMAP assessment tool. This case study will be done with a 360-degree assessment. Your company will provide a list of managers along with their direct reports and supervisors. We will then send the assessment to each one of them. The supervisor and the direct reports will complete the assessment about the manager while each manager will complete a self-assessment.

If your company is interested in participating in this case study, simply fill out the form below which will represent a simple agreement between EMI and your company. We will then contact you and provide you with a spreadsheet where you can list your participants for the case study, and some text that you can send them.

In return, you will get a wealth of information about the management potential of your participants which can help your firm in many ways.

Thank you for your participation, feel free to watch the video above for more information about this process including the 4 key drivers of successful engineering managers which our research has uncovered.

Thank you for your participation.

Anthony Fasano, P.E.

President of Engineering Management Institute
Author of Engineer Your Own Success

Need more information?

Call us at 201.857.2384 or send me an email at afasano@engineeringmanagementinstitute.org.

Email Us

CASE STUDY AGREEMENT

EMI agrees that all of your company and employee information will remain confidential and shall not be used or disclosed to any third party except as authorized herein or by your company in writing. By taking part in this case study, your company grants the right for the EMI to use the information gathered for purposes of global research (e.g., benchmarking), including work that may profit EMI. For the purposes of such research, your company and individual identities will be kept private from anyone not involved in performing work related to the Assessment or related to global research.  Your firm will receive a company specific report based on your participants responses.

[gravityform id="76" name="CMAP Case Study Agreement" title="false" description="false"]

CMAP FAQs

How many do you need to participate?

There is no specific minimum or maximum, however, 3-7 Engineering Managers that you would like to be assessed would work well. Remember, we’ll also need to survey the supervisor of each manager as well as that manager’s reports to get the 360 data. PLEASE NOTE the person being assessed.

At what level of leadership, if any?

Any leadership level is accepted.  As long as someone is managing at least one person, then we can include them.

What is the anticipated timeline of the project?

The case study will run from late April 2019 to end of June 2019.  Then we will make final adjustments to the tool and we expect to launch the final assessment tool by Fall of 2019.  You will receive a report on your participants roughly a month after they take it.

How does the assessment measure the input – ratings/rankings both?

The assessment is based on the research we conducted that has identified the 4 key drivers of successful managers in the engineering field and will be rated on these.  See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. This research was done by an outside data analytics firm and has over 1,000 data points.  For this case study it will be done in a 360 style as described in the answer to question #1 above.

How long does it take to complete?

It will take 7 to 10 minutes to complete the assessment for each individual.

What do the results output look like?

The results output will have the summary of the assessment according to the 4 key drivers we’ve identified in our previous research. See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. It will also provide recommendations that will help the manager reach improve in areas that historical data shows successful engineers excelled in. We will provide you with two sets of data, one that shows all of the participants from your firm, and one that shows your participants versus the global set for the study.

How are the assessments delivered to the participant?

Once we have the names and emails of the participants, they will receive an email for the link to participate in the case study assessment.  We will also provide you with text that you can send them ahead of the assessment going out to prepare them.  The text is very positive and tells them they have been selected to be a part of this groundbreaking research.

How are we expected to use the outcome, hiring/promotions etc.?

That will ultimately be up to you, but here are some areas where the data may be helpful to you. The data can help you to:

  • Identify young engineering professionals that display key drivers of great managers, which can help with promotion or restructuring decisions,
  • See what geographic regions of your company have the highest potential managers,
  • Compare your most profitable departments or teams and see if the most profitable are run by managers that exhibit all or most of the key drivers,
  • Identify specific training needs for each manager so you are sending people to train that they don’t need.
  • Create content in the way of lunch and learns or bolster your internal training programs with relevant content.

Still have questions?

Call us at 201.857.2384 or send me an email at afasano@engineeringmanagementinstitute.org.

Email Us

What if there was an assessment for engineering professionals, different than the DISC or Meyers Briggs? One that could assess the current management abilities of your engineering professionals, to provide them (and you) with valuable information on how and where to improve. Through our relationships with many engineering firms, we’ve recognized that it is nearly impossible for firms to accurately identify which engineers need management training, and on what skillsets. Therefore, many firms are spending money on training without being sure that it is needed, or not focusing their resources on the right people or departments. At the Engineering Management Institute (EMI), we have a goal of solving this problem, and we’d like you to be a part of the solution. We’ve been working on an assessment tool that can determine an engineering professional’s current abilities to be an effective and profitable manager, based on success markers of over one thousand past and current successful engineering managers. And now, we are in the final stages of completing this assessment tool, and we’d like to invite your firm to be part of this exciting process! Introducing the CMAP Assessment Tool… The Current Management Abilities Potential Assessment (CMAP) was built with the help of an outside data analytics company, and a consortium of leading engineering firms across the country. The data used to build the assessment tool was and will be taken from three sources including our hundreds of podcast interviews with successful engineering professionals, a survey of our 15,000-person email list, and a case study that will include a survey of at least 200 current engineering managers, their reports and their managers on their effectiveness as managers. To properly develop this assessment tool, we are looking for firms to be part of the final case study for the CMAP assessment tool. This case study will be done with a 360-degree assessment. Your company will provide a list of managers along with their direct reports and supervisors. We will then send the assessment to each one of them. The supervisor and the direct reports will complete the assessment about the manager while each manager will complete a self-assessment. If your company is interested in participating in this case study, simply fill out the form below which will represent a simple agreement between EMI and your company. We will then contact you and provide you with a spreadsheet where you can list your participants for the case study, and some text that you can send them. In return, you will get a wealth of information about the management potential of your participants which can help your firm in many ways. Thank you for your participation, feel free to watch the video above for more information about this process including the 4 key drivers of successful engineering managers which our research has uncovered. Thank you for your participation.
Anthony Fasano, P.E. President of Engineering Management Institute Author of Engineer Your Own Success

company in writing. By taking part in this case study, your company grants the right for the EMI to use the information gathered for purposes of global research (e.g., benchmarking), including work that may profit EMI. For the purposes of such research, your company and individual identities will be kept private from anyone not involved in performing work related to the Assessment or related to global research. Remember, we’ll also need to survey the supervisor of each manager as well as that manager’s reports to get the 360 data. PLEASE NOTE the person being assessed.";s:27:"flrich1556132070216_content";s:0:"";}i:1;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:36:"At what level of leadership, if any?";s:7:"content";s:113:"Any leadership level is accepted.  As long as someone is managing at least one person, then we can include them.";s:27:"flrich1556132255052_content";s:0:"";}i:2;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:48:"What is the anticipated timeline of the project?";s:7:"content";s:267:"The case study will run from late April 2019 to end of June 2019.  Then we will make final adjustments to the tool and we expect to launch the final assessment tool by Fall of 2019.  You will receive a report on your participants roughly a month after they take it.";s:27:"flrich1556132277580_content";s:0:"";}i:3;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:68:"How does the assessment measure the input – ratings/rankings both?";s:7:"content";s:420:"The assessment is based on the research we conducted that has identified the 4 key drivers of successful managers in the engineering field and will be rated on these.  See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. This research was done by an outside data analytics firm and has over 1,000 data points.  For this case study it will be done in a 360 style as described in the answer to question #1 above.";s:27:"flrich1556132300308_content";s:0:"";}i:4;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:34:"How long does it take to complete?";s:7:"content";s:76:"It will take 7 to 10 minutes to complete the assessment for each individual.";s:27:"flrich1556132321103_content";s:0:"";}i:5;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:37:"What do the results output look like?";s:7:"content";s:515:"The results output will have the summary of the assessment according to the 4 key drivers we’ve identified in our previous research. See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. It will also provide recommendations that will help the manager reach improve in areas that historical data shows successful engineers excelled in. We will provide you with two sets of data, one that shows all of the participants from your firm, and one that shows your participants versus the global set for the study.";s:27:"flrich1556132338257_content";s:0:"";}i:6;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:53:"How are the assessments delivered to the participant?";s:7:"content";s:363:"Once we have the names and emails of the participants, they will receive an email for the link to participate in the case study assessment.  We will also provide you with text that you can send them ahead of the assessment going out to prepare them.  The text is very positive and tells them they have been selected to be a part of this groundbreaking research.";s:27:"flrich1556132370373_content";s:0:"";}i:7;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:63:"How are we expected to use the outcome, hiring/promotions etc.?";s:7:"content";s:780:"That will ultimately be up to you, but here are some areas where the data may be helpful to you. The data can help you to:
  • Identify young engineering professionals that display key drivers of great managers, which can help with promotion or restructuring decisions,
  • See what geographic regions of your company have the highest potential managers,
  • Compare your most profitable departments or teams and see if the most profitable are run by managers that exhibit all or most of the key drivers,
  • Identify specific training needs for each manager so you are sending people to train that they don’t need.
  • Create content in the way of lunch and learns or bolster your internal training programs with relevant content.

What if there was an assessment for engineering professionals, different than the DISC or Meyers Briggs? One that could assess the current management abilities of your engineering professionals, to provide them (and you) with valuable information on how and where to improve. Through our relationships with many engineering firms, we’ve recognized that it is nearly impossible for firms to accurately identify which engineers need management training, and on what skillsets. Therefore, many firms are spending money on training without being sure that it is needed, or not focusing their resources on the right people or departments. At the Engineering Management Institute (EMI), we have a goal of solving this problem, and we’d like you to be a part of the solution. We’ve been working on an assessment tool that can determine an engineering professional’s current abilities to be an effective and profitable manager, based on success markers of over one thousand past and current successful engineering managers. And now, we are in the final stages of completing this assessment tool, and we’d like to invite your firm to be part of this exciting process! Introducing the CMAP Assessment Tool… The Current Management Abilities Potential Assessment (CMAP) was built with the help of an outside data analytics company, and a consortium of leading engineering firms across the country. The data used to build the assessment tool was and will be taken from three sources including our hundreds of podcast interviews with successful engineering professionals, a survey of our 15,000-person email list, and a case study that will include a survey of at least 200 current engineering managers, their reports and their managers on their effectiveness as managers. To properly develop this assessment tool, we are looking for firms to be part of the final case study for the CMAP assessment tool. This case study will be done with a 360-degree assessment. Your company will provide a list of managers along with their direct reports and supervisors. We will then send the assessment to each one of them. The supervisor and the direct reports will complete the assessment about the manager while each manager will complete a self-assessment. If your company is interested in participating in this case study, simply fill out the form below which will represent a simple agreement between EMI and your company. We will then contact you and provide you with a spreadsheet where you can list your participants for the case study, and some text that you can send them. In return, you will get a wealth of information about the management potential of your participants which can help your firm in many ways. Thank you for your participation, feel free to watch the video above for more information about this process including the 4 key drivers of successful engineering managers which our research has uncovered. Thank you for your participation.
Anthony Fasano, P.E. President of Engineering Management Institute Author of Engineer Your Own Success

many do you need to participate?";s:7:"content";s:292:"There is no specific minimum or maximum, however, 3-7 Engineering Managers that you would like to be assessed would work well. Remember, we’ll also need to survey the supervisor of each manager as well as that manager’s reports to get the 360 data. PLEASE NOTE the person being assessed.";s:1:"0";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1556132070216_content";s:0:"";}i:1;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:36:"At what level of leadership, if any?";s:7:"content";s:113:"Any leadership level is accepted.  As long as someone is managing at least one person, then we can include them.";s:27:"flrich1556132255052_content";s:0:"";}i:2;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:48:"What is the anticipated timeline of the project?";s:7:"content";s:267:"The case study will run from late April 2019 to end of June 2019.  Then we will make final adjustments to the tool and we expect to launch the final assessment tool by Fall of 2019.  You will receive a report on your participants roughly a month after they take it.";s:27:"flrich1556132277580_content";s:0:"";}i:3;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:68:"How does the assessment measure the input – ratings/rankings both?";s:7:"content";s:420:"The assessment is based on the research we conducted that has identified the 4 key drivers of successful managers in the engineering field and will be rated on these.  See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. This research was done by an outside data analytics firm and has over 1,000 data points.  For this case study it will be done in a 360 style as described in the answer to question #1 above.";s:27:"flrich1556132300308_content";s:0:"";}i:4;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:34:"How long does it take to complete?";s:7:"content";s:76:"It will take 7 to 10 minutes to complete the assessment for each individual.";s:27:"flrich1556132321103_content";s:0:"";}i:5;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:37:"What do the results output look like?";s:7:"content";s:515:"The results output will have the summary of the assessment according to the 4 key drivers we’ve identified in our previous research. See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. It will also provide recommendations that will help the manager reach improve in areas that historical data shows successful engineers excelled in. We will provide you with two sets of data, one that shows all of the participants from your firm, and one that shows your participants versus the global set for the study.";s:27:"flrich1556132338257_content";s:0:"";}i:6;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:53:"How are the assessments delivered to the participant?";s:7:"content";s:363:"Once we have the names and emails of the participants, they will receive an email for the link to participate in the case study assessment.  We will also provide you with text that you can send them ahead of the assessment going out to prepare them.  The text is very positive and tells them they have been selected to be a part of this groundbreaking research.";s:27:"flrich1556132370373_content";s:0:"";}i:7;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:63:"How are we expected to use the outcome, hiring/promotions etc.?";s:7:"content";s:780:"That will ultimately be up to you, but here are some areas where the data may be helpful to you. The data can help you to:
  • Identify young engineering professionals that display key drivers of great managers, which can help with promotion or restructuring decisions,
  • See what geographic regions of your company have the highest potential managers,
  • Compare your most profitable departments or teams and see if the most profitable are run by managers that exhibit all or most of the key drivers,
  • Identify specific training needs for each manager so you are sending people to train that they don’t need.
  • Create content in the way of lunch and learns or bolster your internal training programs with relevant content.

item.title: Developing Effective Communication Skills for Engineering Professionals (1 PDH) for CALYX Engineers and Consultants
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
Live Webinar for CALYX Engineers and Consultants
Tuesday, June 18th at 12:00 pm EDT

Developing Effective Communication Skills for Engineering Professionals (1 PDH)

If you ask anyone how to be a successful engineer or a top engineering manager, they would tell you that you would need good communication skills. However, they will rarely tell you how to develop them. In this session, you will learn practical strategies that can be used on a daily basis to incrementally improve your communication skills and subsequently your ability to communicate with others. Implementing these strategies will put you on the fast track to becoming a top engineering manager because you will be in the small percentage of engineering professionals who can communicate effectively on a regular basis.

If you follow the strategies provided in this session, this one session alone can have a massively positive impact on your engineering career and life.

In this session, you will learn how to:

  • Communicate effectively with team members,
  • Say things in a way that will help you get the responses you are seeking,
  • Listen more effectively (the key to communication),
  • Be responsive so people want to communicate with you, and
  • Apply strategies to effectively collaborate with your team members.

About Your Speaker:

Anthony Fasano, P.E. is a globally recognized best-selling author, speaker, host of The Civil Engineering Podcast which has been downloaded 1 million times, and author of the bestselling book for engineers entitled Engineer Your Own Success: 7 Key Elements to Creating an Extraordinary Engineering Career (by IEEE-Wiley Press). He is the founder of the Engineering Management Institute which has helped thousands of engineering professionals develop their management and leadership skills.

Live Webinar for CALYX Engineers and Consultants Tuesday, June 18th at 12:00 pm EDT

Developing Effective Communication Skills for Engineering Professionals (1 PDH)

If you ask anyone how to be a successful engineer or a top engineering manager, they would tell you that you would need good communication skills. However, they will rarely tell you how to develop them. In this session, you will learn practical strategies that can be used on a daily basis to incrementally improve your communication skills and subsequently your ability to communicate with others. Implementing these strategies will put you on the fast track to becoming a top engineering manager because you will be in the small percentage of engineering professionals who can communicate effectively on a regular basis. If you follow the strategies provided in this session, this one session alone can have a massively positive impact on your engineering career and life. In this session, you will learn how to:
  • Communicate effectively with team members,
  • Say things in a way that will help you get the responses you are seeking,
  • Listen more effectively (the key to communication),
  • Be responsive so people want to communicate with you, and
  • Apply strategies to effectively collaborate with your team members.
About Your Speaker: Anthony Fasano, P.E. is a globally recognized best-selling author, speaker, host of The Civil Engineering Podcast which has been downloaded 1 million times, and author of the bestselling book for engineers entitled Engineer Your Own Success: 7 Key Elements to Creating an Extraordinary Engineering Career (by IEEE-Wiley Press). He is the founder of the Engineering Management Institute which has helped thousands of engineering professionals develop their management and leadership Live Webinar for CALYX Engineers and Consultants Tuesday, June 18th at 12:00 pm EDT

Developing Effective Communication Skills for Engineering Professionals (1 PDH)

If you ask anyone how to be a successful engineer or a top engineering manager, they would tell you that you would need good communication skills. However, they will rarely tell you how to develop them. In this session, you will learn practical strategies that can be used on a daily basis to incrementally improve your communication skills and subsequently your ability to communicate with others. Implementing these strategies will put you on the fast track to becoming a top engineering manager because you will be in the small percentage of engineering professionals who can communicate effectively on a regular basis. If you follow the strategies provided in this session, this one session alone can have a massively positive impact on your engineering career and life. In this session, you will learn how to:
  • Communicate effectively with team members,
  • Say things in a way that will help you get the responses you are seeking,
  • Listen more effectively (the key to communication),
  • Be responsive so people want to communicate with you, and
  • Apply strategies to effectively collaborate with your team members.
About Your Speaker: Anthony Fasano, P.E. is a globally recognized best-selling author, speaker, host of The Civil Engineering Podcast which has been downloaded 1 million times, and author of the bestselling book for engineers entitled Engineer Your Own Success: 7 Key Elements to Creating an Extraordinary Engineering Career (by IEEE-Wiley Press). He is the founder of the Engineering Management Institute which has helped thousands of engineering professionals develop their management and leadership skills.";s:5:"color";s:0:"";s:10:"typography";a:11:{s:11:"font_family";s:7:"Default";s:11:"font_weight";s:7:"default";s:9:"font_size";a:2:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";s:4:"unit";s:2:"px";}s:11:"line_height";a:2:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";s:4:"unit";s:0:"";}s:10:"text_align";s:0:"";s:14:"letter_spacing";a:1:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";}s:14:"text_transform";s:0:"";s:15:"text_decoration";s:0:"";s:10:"font_style";s:0:"";s:12:"font_variant";s:0:"";s:11:"text_shadow";a:4:{s:5:"color";s:0:"";s:10:"horizontal";s:0:"";s:8:"vertical";s:0:"";s:4:"blur";s:0:"";}}s:17:"typography_medium";a:9:{s:9:"font_size";a:2:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";s:4:"unit";s:2:"px";}s:11:"line_height";a:2:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";s:4:"unit";s:0:"";}s:10:"text_align";s:0:"";s:14:"letter_spacing";a:1:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";}s:14:"text_transform";s:0:"";s:15:"text_decoration";s:0:"";s:10:"font_style";s:0:"";s:12:"font_variant";s:0:"";s:11:"text_shadow";a:4:{s:5:"color";s:0:"";s:10:"horizontal";s:0:"";s:8:"vertical";s:0:"";s:4:"blur";s:0:"";}}s:21:"typography_responsive";a:9:{s:9:"font_size";a:2:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";s:4:"unit";s:2:"px";}s:11:"line_height";a:2:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";s:4:"unit";s:0:"";}s:10:"text_align";s:0:"";s:14:"letter_spacing";a:1:{s:6:"length";s:0:"";}s:14:"text_transform";s:0:"";s:15:"text_decoration";s:0:"";s:10:"font_style";s:0:"";s:12:"font_variant";s:0:"";s:11:"text_shadow";a:4:{s:5:"color";s:0:"";s:10:"horizontal";s:0:"";s:8:"vertical";s:0:"";s:4:"blur";s:0:"";}}s:18:"responsive_display";s:0:"";s:18:"visibility_display";s:0:"";s:26:"visibility_user_capability";s:0:"";s:9:"animation";a:3:{s:5:"style";s:0:"";s:5:"delay";s:3:"0.0";s:8:"duration";s:1:"1";}s:17:"container_element";s:3:"div";s:2:"id";s:0:"";s:5:"class";s:0:"";s:10:"margin_top";s:0:"";s:11:"margin_unit";s:2:"px";s:17:"margin_top_medium";s:0:"";s:18:"margin_medium_unit";s:2:"px";s:21:"margin_top_responsive";s:0:"";s:22:"margin_responsive_unit";s:2:"px";s:12:"margin_right";s:0:"";s:19:"margin_right_medium";s:0:"";s:23:"margin_right_responsive";s:0:"";s:13 How to Use LinkedIn Effectively to Build Your Professional Network Webinar Recording

Engineering Manager Case Study, please call 201-857-2384.

item.title: Group Coaching Call (2) for AI Engineers Participants Recording
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
Group Coaching Call 2 Presentation for AI Engineers Participants Recording

[embed]https://vimeo.com/339625041[/embed]

Please complete the following session evaluations if you need or don't need PDH credits:

  • Developing Effective Communication Skills
  • Building Expertise and Mastering Public Speaking
  • Maximizing Billability and Productivity for Engineering Professionals
  • How to Network and Build Relationships for Engineering Professionals
  • Developing the Engineering Leader Inside of You by Engaging the People You Lead

item.title: Digital Badges
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
Engineering Management Institute Digital Badge Program

The Engineering Management Institute has partnered with digital badging provider Credly through their Acclaim platform to provide our certification holders with a digital badge of their EMI certifications. Digital badges can be used in email signatures, personal websites, and social media sites such as LinkedIn and Twitter. This digital image contains verified metadata that describes the qualifications and the process required to earn them. 

Benefits to Individuals

  • Validate your skills. Stand out.
  • Earn recognition for your knowledge.
  • Increase your value to your employer.
  • Enhance your credibility as an engineering professional.
  • Get a digital badge you can share.

Benefits to Employers

  • Secure a competitive edge.
  • Transform your talent into certified professionals.
  • Maximize your investment in EMI.
  • Make your business smarter and more innovative.
  • Develop excellence for a competitive edge.
  • Provide a defined training path for employees.

How It Works

When you earn an EMI certification, you'll also receive an email from Acclaim inviting you to claim your badge.

  • Click the link in the email.
  • Create an account on the Acclaim site.
  • Accept your badge through an email from Acclaim and start sharing.

We hope you will take advantage of this new functionality and enjoy the convenience and professional recognition afforded by our new badging program.

If you do not receive an email from Acclaim within two weeks after being certified, let us know at Betty@engineeringmanagementinstitute.org.

Digital Badges FAQs

What is an open badge?

Open badges are web-enabled versions of a credential, certification or learning outcome which can be verified in real-time, online.

How does my certification get displayed as a badge?

We have partnered with Credly to translate the learning outcomes you’ve demonstrated into a badge, issued and managed through the company’s Acclaim Platform. The technology Credly uses on its Acclaim Platform is based on the Open Badge Standards maintained by IMS Global. This enables you to manage, share and verify your competencies digitally.

What are the benefits of a badge?

Representing your skills as a badge gives you a way to share your abilities online in a way that is simple, trusted and can be easily verified in real time. Badges provide employers and peers concrete evidence of what you had to do to earn your credential and what you’re now capable of. Credly’s Acclaim Platform also offers labor market insights, based on your skills. You can search and apply for job opportunities right through the Acclaim Platform.

Who is Credly?

Credly is the end-to-end solution for issuing and managing digital credentials. Credly works with credible organizations to provide digital credentials to individuals, worldwide.

How will I know if I’ve earned a badge?

You will receive an email notification from the Acclaim Platform (admin@youracclaim.com) with instructions for claiming your badge and setting up your account so please white list this email address to ensure smooth delivery of necessary set up and account notices.

What if I don’t want my badge to be public?

You can easily configure your privacy settings in the Acclaim Platform. You’re in complete control of the information about yourself that is made public.

Is there a fee to use Credly’s Acclaim Platform?

No. This is a service we provide to you, at no cost.

What’s to keep someone else from copying my badge and using it?

While badges are simply digital image files, they are uniquely linked to data hosted on Credly’s Acclaim Platform. This link to verified data makes them more reliable and secure than a paper-based certificate. It also eliminates the possibility of anyone claiming your credential and your associated identity.

Where and how can I share my badge through Credly’s Acclaim Platform?

You can share your badge directly from the Acclaim Platform to LinkedIn, Twitter and Facebook; over email; embedded in a website or in your email signature.

Email Signature

  • Log in to your Acclaim account, navigate to your profile, right-click on your badge image, and copy.
  • Within your email settings, access your email signature and paste the badge image into your signature.
  • Back in Acclaim, copy the badge landing page link.
  • In your email signature, highlight your badge and hyperlink with the link you just copied.

Please note: Your email client may require a different process for adding an image to your signature. Please contact your email client administrator with any questions.

LinkedIn

LinkedIn requires individuals to add information about their certification in order to share their badge to their profile. To share your badge details to your LinkedIn profile, view the detailed instructions listed within Acclaim.

Facebook and Twitter

  • Click the sharing icon from Acclaim.
  • Be prompted by Facebook or Twitter to connect your account – this gives Facebook and Twitter permission to share your badge from Acclaim.

The badge (image and all) will show up in your Twitter timeline or on your Facebook profile.

item.title: EMI Training Programs
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
CUSTOMIZED TRAINING

We offer a variety of online and in-person training events/courses and content on the following topics.  Click on your topic of interest for more information.  You can also contact us at (800) 920-4007 ext. 800 or by submitting a request through this form for customized events. As of 2019, EMI will issue Digital Badges to all our Accelerator/Training program participants.

People Skills & Leadership

People Management Customized Training

Project Management

 

Project Management Accelerator Online Workshop

Licensure Exam Review

PPI - Kaplan Company

EMI Digital Badging Program

Stand out from the rest. You've earned it.

When you or your employees complete an Accelerator/training program from EMI, you also earn a digital badge that you can share on your social media sites. Click here to learn more.

Are you ready to find out how we can help you and your company succeed?

Call us at 201.857.2384 for more information about our training programs.

Get Started Today!

item.title: Recording - Maximizing Billability and Productivity for Engineering Professionals
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
Maximizing Billability and Productivity for Engineering Professionals Webinar Recording

If your company is interested in participating in the
Engineering Manager Case Study, please call 201-857-2384.

item.title: What is CMAP Assessment
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
The CMAP Diagnostics Case Study and the
4 Key Drivers of Successful Engineering Managers...

What if there was a diagnostic tool for engineering professionals, different than the DISC or Meyers Briggs? One that could assess the current management abilities of your engineering professionals, to provide them (and you) with valuable information on how and where to improve.

Through our relationships with many engineering firms, we’ve recognized that it is nearly impossible for firms to accurately identify which engineers need management training, and on what skillsets. Therefore, many firms are spending money on training without being sure that it is needed, or not focusing their resources on the right people or departments.

At the Engineering Management Institute (EMI), we have a goal of solving this problem, and we’d like you to be a part of the solution. We’ve been working on a diagnostic tool that can determine an engineering professional’s current abilities to be an effective and profitable manager, based on success markers of over one thousand past and current successful engineering managers.

And now, we are in the final stages of completing this diagnostic tool, and we’d like to invite you and your firm to be part of this exciting process!

Introducing the CMAP Diagnostic Tool…

The Current Management Abilities Potential Diagnostics (CMAP) was built with the help of an outside data analytics company, and a consortium of leading engineering firms across the country.

The data used to build the diagnostic tool was and will be taken from three sources including our hundreds of podcast interviews with successful engineering professionals, a survey of our 15,000-person email list, and a case study that will include a survey of at least 200 current engineering managers, their reports and their managers on their effectiveness as managers.

To properly develop this diagnostic tool, we are looking for firms to be part of the final case study for the CMAP diagnostic tool. This case study will be done with a 360-degree process. Your company will provide a list of managers along with their direct reports and supervisors. We will then send the survey to each one of them. The supervisor and the direct reports will complete the survey about the manager while each manager will complete a self-diagnostics.

If your company is interested in participating in this case study, simply fill out the form below which will represent a simple agreement between EMI and your company. We will then contact you and provide you with a spreadsheet where you can list your participants for the case study, and some text that you can send them.

In return, you will get a wealth of information about the management potential of your participants which can help your firm in many ways.

Thank you for your participation, feel free to watch the video above for more information about this process including the 4 key drivers of successful engineering managers which our research has uncovered.

Thank you for your participation.

Anthony Fasano, P.E.

President of Engineering Management Institute
Author of Engineer Your Own Success

Need more information?

Call us at 201.857.2384 or send me an email at afasano@engineeringmanagementinstitute.org.

Email Us

CASE STUDY AGREEMENT

EMI agrees that all of your company and employee information will remain confidential and shall not be used or disclosed to any third party except as authorized herein or by your company in writing. By taking part in this case study, your company grants the right for the EMI to use the information gathered for purposes of global research (e.g., benchmarking), including work that may profit EMI. For the purposes of such research, your company and individual identities will be kept private from anyone not involved in performing work related to the Diagnostics or related to global research.  You firm will receive a report based on the participants' responses.

[gravityform id="76" name="CMAP Case Study Agreement" title="false" description="false"]

CMAP FAQs

How many do you need to participate?

There is no specific minimum or maximum, however, 3-7 Engineering Managers that you would like to be assessed would work well. Remember, we’ll also need to survey the supervisor of each manager as well as that manager’s reports to get the 360 data. PLEASE NOTE the person being assessed.

At what level of leadership, if any?

Any leadership level is accepted.  As long as someone is managing at least one person, then we can include them.

What is the anticipated timeline of the project?

The case study will run from late April 2019 to end of July 2019.  Then we will make final adjustments to the tool and we expect to launch the final diagnostic tool hopefully by Fall of 2019.  You will receive a report roughly a month after they take it or after we have collected enough data points.

How does the diagnostics measure the input – ratings/rankings both?

The diagnostics is based on the research we conducted that has identified the 4 key drivers of successful managers in the engineering field and will be rated on these.  See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. This research was done by an outside data analytics firm and has over 1,000 data points.  For this case study it will be done in a 360 style as described in the answer to question #1 above.

How long does it take to complete?

It will take about 10-15 minutes to complete the diagnostics for each individual.

What do the results output look like?

The results output will have the summary of the diagnostics according to the 4 key drivers we’ve identified in our previous research. See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. It will also provide recommendations that will help the manager reach improve in areas that historical data shows successful engineers excelled in. We will provide you with two sets of data, one that shows all of the participants from your firm, and one that shows your participants versus the global set for the study.

How are the results delivered to the participant?

Once we have the names and emails of the participants, they will receive an email for the link to participate in the case study diagnostics.  We will also provide you with text that you can send them ahead of the diagnostics going out to prepare them.  The text is very positive and tells them they have been selected to be a part of this groundbreaking research.

How are we expected to use the outcome, hiring/promotions etc.?

That will ultimately be up to you, but here are some areas where the data may be helpful to you. The data can help you to:

  • Identify young engineering professionals that display key drivers of great managers, which can help with promotion or restructuring decisions,
  • See what geographic regions of your company have the highest potential managers,
  • Compare your most profitable departments or teams and see if the most profitable are run by managers that exhibit all or most of the key drivers,
  • Identify specific training needs for each manager so you are sending people to train that they don’t need.
  • Create content in the way of lunch and learns or bolster your internal training programs with relevant content.

Still have questions?

Call us at 201.857.2384 or send me an email at afasano@engineeringmanagementinstitute.org.

Email Us

What if there was a diagnostic tool for engineering professionals, different than the DISC or Meyers Briggs? One that could assess the current management abilities of your engineering professionals, to provide them (and you) with valuable information on how and where to improve. Through our relationships with many engineering firms, we’ve recognized that it is nearly impossible for firms to accurately identify which engineers need management training, and on what skillsets. Therefore, many firms are spending money on training without being sure that it is needed, or not focusing their resources on the right people or departments. At the Engineering Management Institute (EMI), we have a goal of solving this problem, and we’d like you to be a part of the solution. We’ve been working on a diagnostic tool that can determine an engineering professional’s current abilities to be an effective and profitable manager, based on success markers of over one thousand past and current successful engineering managers. And now, we are in the final stages of completing this diagnostic tool, and we’d like to invite you and your firm to be part of this exciting process! Introducing the CMAP Diagnostic Tool… The Current Management Abilities Potential Diagnostics (CMAP) was built with the help of an outside data analytics company, and a consortium of leading engineering firms across the country. The data used to build the diagnostic tool was and will be taken from three sources including our hundreds of podcast interviews with successful engineering professionals, a survey of our 15,000-person email list, and a case study that will include a survey of at least 200 current engineering managers, their reports and their managers on their effectiveness as managers. To properly develop this diagnostic tool, we are looking for firms to be part of the final case study for the CMAP diagnostic tool. This case study will be done with a 360-degree process. Your company will provide a list of managers along with their direct reports and supervisors. We will then send the survey to each one of them. The supervisor and the direct reports will complete the survey about the manager while each manager will complete a self-diagnostics. If your company is interested in participating in this case study, simply fill out the form below which will represent a simple agreement between EMI and your company. We will then contact you and provide you with a spreadsheet where you can list your participants for the case study, and some text that you can send them. In return, you will get a wealth of information about the management potential of your participants which can help your firm in many ways. Thank you for your participation, feel free to watch the video above for more information about this process including the 4 key drivers of successful engineering managers which our research has uncovered. Thank you for your participation. Anthony Fasano, P.E. President of Engineering Management Institute Author of Engineer Your Own Success

"position";i:0;s:8:"settings";s:0:"";}s:13:"5cc0b0e11a966";O:8:"stdClass":5:{s:4:"node";s:13:"5cc0b0e11a966";s:4:"type";s:6:"column";s:6:"parent";s:13:"5cc0b0e11a861";s:8:"position";i:0;s:8:"settings";O:8:"stdClass":80:{s:4:"size";i:100;s:11:"size_medium";s:0:"";s:15:"size_responsive";s:0:"";s:10:"min_height";s:0:"";s:15:"min_height_unit";s:2:"px";s:17:"min_height_medium";s:0:"";s:22:"min_height_medium_unit";s:2:"px";s:21:"min_height_responsive";s:0:"";s:26:"min_height_responsive_unit";s:2:"px";s:12:"equal_height";s:2:"no";s:17:"content_alignment";s:3:"top";s:10:"text_color";s:0:"";s:10:"link_color";s:0:"";s:11:"hover_color";s:0:"";s:13:"heading_color";s:0:"";s:7:"bg_type";s:5:"color";s:8:"bg_color";s:0:"";s:11:"bg_gradient";s:0:"";s:8:"bg_image";s:0:"";s:12:"bg_image_src";s:0:"";s:15:"bg_image_medium";s:0:"";s:19:"bg_image_medium_src";s:0:"";s:19:"bg_image_responsive";s:0:"";s:23:"bg_image_responsive_src";s:0:"";s:9:"bg_repeat";s:4:"none";s:16:"bg_repeat_medium";s:0:"";s:20:"bg_repeat_responsive";s:0:"";s:11:"bg_position";s:13:"center center";s:18:"bg_position_medium";s:0:"";s:22:"bg_position_responsive";s:0:"";s:13:"bg_attachment";s:6:"scroll";s:20:"bg_attachment_medium";s:0:"";s:24:"bg_attachment_responsive";s:0:"";s:7:"bg_size";s:5:"cover";s:14:"bg_size_medium";s:0:"";s:18:"bg_size_responsive";s:0:"";s:15:"bg_overlay_type";s:5:"color";s:16:"bg_overlay_color";s:0:"";s:19:"bg_overlay_gradient";s:0:"";s:6:"border";s:0:"";s:13:"border_medium";s:0:"";s:17:"border_responsive";s:0:"";s:18:"responsive_display";s:0:"";s:16:"responsive_order";s:7:"default";s:18:"visibility_display";s:0:"";s:26:"visibility_user_capability";s:0:"";s:9:"animation";s:0:"";s:17:"container_element";s:3:"div";s:2:"id";s:0:"";s:5:"class";s:0:"";s:10:"margin_top";s:0:"";s:11:"margin_unit";s:2:"px";s:17:"margin_top_medium";s:0:"";s:18:"margin_medium_unit";s:2:"px";s:21:"margin_top_responsive";s:0:"";s:22:"margin_responsive_unit";s:2:"px";s:12:"margin_right";s:0:"";s:19:"margin_right_medium";s:0:"";s:23:"margin_right_responsive";s:0:"";s:13:"margin_bottom";s:0:"";s:20:"margin_bottom_medium";s:0:"";s:24:"margin_bottom_responsive";s:0:"";s:11:"margin_left";s:0:"";s:18:"margin_left_medium";s:0:"";s:22:"margin_left_responsive";s:0:"";s:11:"padding_top";s:0:"";s:12:"padding_unit";s:2:"px";s:18:"padding_top_medium";s:0:"";s:19:"padding_medium_unit";s:2:"px";s:22:"padding_top_responsive";s:0:"";s:23:"padding_responsive_unit";s:2:"px";s:13:"padding_right";s:0:"";s:20:"padding_right_medium";s:0:"";s:24:"padding_right_responsive";s:0:"";s:14:"padding_bottom";s:0:"";s:21:"padding_bottom_medium";s:0:"";s:25:"padding_bottom_responsive";s:0:"";s:12:"padding_left";s:0:"";s:19:"padding_left_medium";s:0:"";s:23:"padding_left_responsive";s:0:"";}}s:13:"5cc0b0e11339a";O:8:"stdClass":5:{s:4:"node";s:13:"5cc0b0e11339a";s:4:"type";s:6:"module";s:6:"parent";s:13:"5cc0b0e11a966";s:8:"position";i:0;s:8:"settings";O:8:"stdClass":76:{s:5:"items";a:8:{i:0;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:36:"How many do you need to participate?";s:7:"content";s:292:"There is no specific minimum or maximum, however, 3-7 Engineering Managers that you would like to be assessed would work well. Remember, we’ll also need to survey the supervisor of each manager as well as that manager’s reports to get the 360 data. PLEASE NOTE the person being assessed.";s:1:"0";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1556132070216_content";s:0:"";}i:1;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:36:"At what level of leadership, if any?";s:7:"content";s:113:"Any leadership level is accepted.  As long as someone is managing at least one person, then we can include them.";s:27:"flrich1556132255052_content";s:0:"";}i:2;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:48:"What is the anticipated timeline of the project?";s:7:"content";s:302:"The case study will run from late April 2019 to end of July 2019.  Then we will make final adjustments to the tool and we expect to launch the final diagnostic tool hopefully by Fall of 2019.  You will receive a report roughly a month after they take it or after we have collected enough data points.";s:27:"flrich1556132277580_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308485318_content";s:0:"";}i:3;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:69:"How does the diagnostics measure the input – ratings/rankings both?";s:7:"content";s:421:"The diagnostics is based on the research we conducted that has identified the 4 key drivers of successful managers in the engineering field and will be rated on these.  See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. This research was done by an outside data analytics firm and has over 1,000 data points.  For this case study it will be done in a 360 style as described in the answer to question #1 above.";s:27:"flrich1556132300308_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308541264_content";s:0:"";}i:4;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:34:"How long does it take to complete?";s:7:"content";s:81:"It will take about 10-15 minutes to complete the diagnostics for each individual.";s:27:"flrich1556132321103_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308568978_content";s:0:"";}i:5;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:37:"What do the results output look like?";s:7:"content";s:516:"The results output will have the summary of the diagnostics according to the 4 key drivers we’ve identified in our previous research. See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. It will also provide recommendations that will help the manager reach improve in areas that historical data shows successful engineers excelled in. We will provide you with two sets of data, one that shows all of the participants from your firm, and one that shows your participants versus the global set for the study.";s:27:"flrich1556132338257_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308594870_content";s:0:"";}i:6;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:49:"How are the results delivered to the participant?";s:7:"content";s:365:"Once we have the names and emails of the participants, they will receive an email for the link to participate in the case study diagnostics.  We will also provide you with text that you can send them ahead of the diagnostics going out to prepare them.  The text is very positive and tells them they have been selected to be a part of this groundbreaking research.";s:27:"flrich1556132370373_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308615679_content";s:0:"";}i:7;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:63:"How are we expected to use the outcome, hiring/promotions etc.?";s:7:"content";s:780:"That will ultimately be up to you, but here are some areas where the data may be helpful to you. The data can help you to:
  • Identify young engineering professionals that display key drivers of great managers, which can help with promotion or restructuring decisions,
  • See what geographic regions of your company have the highest potential managers,
  • Compare your most profitable departments or teams and see if the most profitable are run by managers that exhibit all or most of the key drivers,
  • Identify specific training needs for each manager so you are sending people to train that they don’t need.
  • Create content in the way of lunch and learns or bolster your internal training programs with relevant content.

What if there was a diagnostic tool for engineering professionals, different than the DISC or Meyers Briggs? One that could assess the current management abilities of your engineering professionals, to provide them (and you) with valuable information on how and where to improve. Through our relationships with many engineering firms, we’ve recognized that it is nearly impossible for firms to accurately identify which engineers need management training, and on what skillsets. Therefore, many firms are spending money on training without being sure that it is needed, or not focusing their resources on the right people or departments. At the Engineering Management Institute (EMI), we have a goal of solving this problem, and we’d like you to be a part of the solution. We’ve been working on a diagnostic tool that can determine an engineering professional’s current abilities to be an effective and profitable manager, based on success markers of over one thousand past and current successful engineering managers. And now, we are in the final stages of completing this diagnostic tool, and we’d like to invite you and your firm to be part of this exciting process! Introducing the CMAP Diagnostic Tool… The Current Management Abilities Potential Diagnostics (CMAP) was built with the help of an outside data analytics company, and a consortium of leading engineering firms across the country. The data used to build the diagnostic tool was and will be taken from three sources including our hundreds of podcast interviews with successful engineering professionals, a survey of our 15,000-person email list, and a case study that will include a survey of at least 200 current engineering managers, their reports and their managers on their effectiveness as managers. To properly develop this diagnostic tool, we are looking for firms to be part of the final case study for the CMAP diagnostic tool. This case study will be done with a 360-degree process. Your company will provide a list of managers along with their direct reports and supervisors. We will then send the survey to each one of them. The supervisor and the direct reports will complete the survey about the manager while each manager will complete a self-diagnostics. If your company is interested in participating in this case study, simply fill out the form below which will represent a simple agreement between EMI and your company. We will then contact you and provide you with a spreadsheet where you can list your participants for the case study, and some text that you can send them. In return, you will get a wealth of information about the management potential of your participants which can help your firm in many ways. Thank you for your participation, feel free to watch the video above for more information about this process including the 4 key drivers of successful engineering managers which our research has uncovered. Thank you for your participation. Anthony Fasano, P.E. President of Engineering Management Institute Author of Engineer Your Own Success

"position";i:0;s:8:"settings";s:0:"";}s:13:"5cc0b0e11a966";O:8:"stdClass":5:{s:4:"node";s:13:"5cc0b0e11a966";s:4:"type";s:6:"column";s:6:"parent";s:13:"5cc0b0e11a861";s:8:"position";i:0;s:8:"settings";O:8:"stdClass":80:{s:4:"size";i:100;s:11:"size_medium";s:0:"";s:15:"size_responsive";s:0:"";s:10:"min_height";s:0:"";s:15:"min_height_unit";s:2:"px";s:17:"min_height_medium";s:0:"";s:22:"min_height_medium_unit";s:2:"px";s:21:"min_height_responsive";s:0:"";s:26:"min_height_responsive_unit";s:2:"px";s:12:"equal_height";s:2:"no";s:17:"content_alignment";s:3:"top";s:10:"text_color";s:0:"";s:10:"link_color";s:0:"";s:11:"hover_color";s:0:"";s:13:"heading_color";s:0:"";s:7:"bg_type";s:5:"color";s:8:"bg_color";s:0:"";s:11:"bg_gradient";s:0:"";s:8:"bg_image";s:0:"";s:12:"bg_image_src";s:0:"";s:15:"bg_image_medium";s:0:"";s:19:"bg_image_medium_src";s:0:"";s:19:"bg_image_responsive";s:0:"";s:23:"bg_image_responsive_src";s:0:"";s:9:"bg_repeat";s:4:"none";s:16:"bg_repeat_medium";s:0:"";s:20:"bg_repeat_responsive";s:0:"";s:11:"bg_position";s:13:"center center";s:18:"bg_position_medium";s:0:"";s:22:"bg_position_responsive";s:0:"";s:13:"bg_attachment";s:6:"scroll";s:20:"bg_attachment_medium";s:0:"";s:24:"bg_attachment_responsive";s:0:"";s:7:"bg_size";s:5:"cover";s:14:"bg_size_medium";s:0:"";s:18:"bg_size_responsive";s:0:"";s:15:"bg_overlay_type";s:5:"color";s:16:"bg_overlay_color";s:0:"";s:19:"bg_overlay_gradient";s:0:"";s:6:"border";s:0:"";s:13:"border_medium";s:0:"";s:17:"border_responsive";s:0:"";s:18:"responsive_display";s:0:"";s:16:"responsive_order";s:7:"default";s:18:"visibility_display";s:0:"";s:26:"visibility_user_capability";s:0:"";s:9:"animation";s:0:"";s:17:"container_element";s:3:"div";s:2:"id";s:0:"";s:5:"class";s:0:"";s:10:"margin_top";s:0:"";s:11:"margin_unit";s:2:"px";s:17:"margin_top_medium";s:0:"";s:18:"margin_medium_unit";s:2:"px";s:21:"margin_top_responsive";s:0:"";s:22:"margin_responsive_unit";s:2:"px";s:12:"margin_right";s:0:"";s:19:"margin_right_medium";s:0:"";s:23:"margin_right_responsive";s:0:"";s:13:"margin_bottom";s:0:"";s:20:"margin_bottom_medium";s:0:"";s:24:"margin_bottom_responsive";s:0:"";s:11:"margin_left";s:0:"";s:18:"margin_left_medium";s:0:"";s:22:"margin_left_responsive";s:0:"";s:11:"padding_top";s:0:"";s:12:"padding_unit";s:2:"px";s:18:"padding_top_medium";s:0:"";s:19:"padding_medium_unit";s:2:"px";s:22:"padding_top_responsive";s:0:"";s:23:"padding_responsive_unit";s:2:"px";s:13:"padding_right";s:0:"";s:20:"padding_right_medium";s:0:"";s:24:"padding_right_responsive";s:0:"";s:14:"padding_bottom";s:0:"";s:21:"padding_bottom_medium";s:0:"";s:25:"padding_bottom_responsive";s:0:"";s:12:"padding_left";s:0:"";s:19:"padding_left_medium";s:0:"";s:23:"padding_left_responsive";s:0:"";}}s:13:"5cc0b0e11339a";O:8:"stdClass":5:{s:4:"node";s:13:"5cc0b0e11339a";s:4:"type";s:6:"module";s:6:"parent";s:13:"5cc0b0e11a966";s:8:"position";i:0;s:8:"settings";O:8:"stdClass":76:{s:5:"items";a:8:{i:0;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:36:"How many do you need to participate?";s:7:"content";s:292:"There is no specific minimum or maximum, however, 3-7 Engineering Managers that you would like to be assessed would work well. Remember, we’ll also need to survey the supervisor of each manager as well as that manager’s reports to get the 360 data. PLEASE NOTE the person being assessed.";s:1:"0";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1556132070216_content";s:0:"";}i:1;O:8:"stdClass":3:{s:5:"label";s:36:"At what level of leadership, if any?";s:7:"content";s:113:"Any leadership level is accepted.  As long as someone is managing at least one person, then we can include them.";s:27:"flrich1556132255052_content";s:0:"";}i:2;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:48:"What is the anticipated timeline of the project?";s:7:"content";s:302:"The case study will run from late April 2019 to end of July 2019.  Then we will make final adjustments to the tool and we expect to launch the final diagnostic tool hopefully by Fall of 2019.  You will receive a report roughly a month after they take it or after we have collected enough data points.";s:27:"flrich1556132277580_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308485318_content";s:0:"";}i:3;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:69:"How does the diagnostics measure the input – ratings/rankings both?";s:7:"content";s:421:"The diagnostics is based on the research we conducted that has identified the 4 key drivers of successful managers in the engineering field and will be rated on these.  See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. This research was done by an outside data analytics firm and has over 1,000 data points.  For this case study it will be done in a 360 style as described in the answer to question #1 above.";s:27:"flrich1556132300308_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308541264_content";s:0:"";}i:4;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:34:"How long does it take to complete?";s:7:"content";s:81:"It will take about 10-15 minutes to complete the diagnostics for each individual.";s:27:"flrich1556132321103_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308568978_content";s:0:"";}i:5;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:37:"What do the results output look like?";s:7:"content";s:516:"The results output will have the summary of the diagnostics according to the 4 key drivers we’ve identified in our previous research. See the portion of the video on this page from 1:55 to 2:42. It will also provide recommendations that will help the manager reach improve in areas that historical data shows successful engineers excelled in. We will provide you with two sets of data, one that shows all of the participants from your firm, and one that shows your participants versus the global set for the study.";s:27:"flrich1556132338257_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308594870_content";s:0:"";}i:6;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:49:"How are the results delivered to the participant?";s:7:"content";s:365:"Once we have the names and emails of the participants, they will receive an email for the link to participate in the case study diagnostics.  We will also provide you with text that you can send them ahead of the diagnostics going out to prepare them.  The text is very positive and tells them they have been selected to be a part of this groundbreaking research.";s:27:"flrich1556132370373_content";s:0:"";s:27:"flrich1560308615679_content";s:0:"";}i:7;O:8:"stdClass":4:{s:5:"label";s:63:"How are we expected to use the outcome, hiring/promotions etc.?";s:7:"content";s:780:"That will ultimately be up to you, but here are some areas where the data may be helpful to you. The data can help you to:
  • Identify young engineering professionals that display key drivers of great managers, which can help with promotion or restructuring decisions,
  • See what geographic regions of your company have the highest potential managers,
  • Compare your most profitable departments or teams and see if the most profitable are run by managers that exhibit all or most of the key drivers,
  • Identify specific training needs for each manager so you are sending people to train that they don’t need.
  • Create content in the way of lunch and learns or bolster your internal training programs with relevant content.

Self-Care and Work-Life Balance: Keys to Engineering Career (and Life) Success Webinar Recording

Self-Care and Work-Life Balance: Keys to Engineering Career (and Life) Success Handout

Click here for the Session Evaluation (If you need a PDH)

If your company is interested in participating in the
Engineering Manager Case Study, please call 201-857-2384.

item.title: Recording - Developing the Engineering Leader Inside of You by Engaging the People You Lead
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
Developing the Engineering Leader Inside of You by Engaging the People You Lead Webinar Recording

If your company is interested in participating in the
Engineering Manager Case Study, please call 201-857-2384.

item.title: Developing the Engineering Leader Inside of You by Engaging the People You Lead for Environmental Partners Group
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
Live Webinar for Environmental Partners Group Engineers and Consultants
Monday, October 21st at 12:30 pm EDT

Developing the Engineering Leader Inside of You by Engaging the People You Lead (1 PDH)

Engineering professionals are rarely taught management and leadership skills in school. They have to learn these skills on the job or suffer the consequences. One of the key components of leadership in engineering is PEOPLE. Great leaders know how to engage and inspire people.

In this session, you will learn how to implement specific strategies that can help you maximize your relationships with the people you work with, like the power of positivity, the importance of open communication with your staff, understanding your role as a leader, and effective delegation. This session will also cover the theory of high output management.

This session will:

  • Help you to realize that you are already a leader,
  • Discuss the power of positivity in leadership,
  • Provide strategies for seeing opportunities in every situation, specifically a two-question framework,
  • Give recommendations for utilizing leverage and delegating the right tasks, and
  • Discuss the importance of earning respect from your peers and how to best engage employees and maximize their potential.

About Your Speaker:

Anthony Fasano, P.E. is a globally recognized best-selling author, speaker, host of The Civil Engineering Podcast which has been downloaded 1 million times, and author of the bestselling book for engineers entitled Engineer Your Own Success: 7 Key Elements to Creating an Extraordinary Engineering Career (by IEEE-Wiley Press). He is the founder of the Engineering Management Institute which has helped thousands of engineering professionals develop their management and leadership skills.

Live Webinar for Environmental Partners Group Engineers and Consultants Monday, October 21st at 12:30 pm EDT

Developing the Engineering Leader Inside of You by Engaging the People You Lead (1 PDH)

Engineering professionals are rarely taught management and leadership skills in school. They have to learn these skills on the job or suffer the consequences. One of the key components of leadership in engineering is PEOPLE. Great leaders know how to engage and inspire people. In this session, you will learn how to implement specific strategies that can help you maximize your relationships with the people you work with, like the power of positivity, the importance of open communication with your staff, understanding your role as a leader, and effective delegation. This session will also cover the theory of high output management. This session will:
  • Help you to realize that you are already a leader,
  • Discuss the power of positivity in leadership,
  • Provide strategies for seeing opportunities in every situation, specifically a two-question framework,
  • Give recommendations for utilizing leverage and delegating the right tasks, and
  • Discuss the importance of earning respect from your peers and how to best engage employees and maximize their potential.
About Your Speaker: Anthony Fasano, P.E. is a globally recognized best-selling author, speaker, host of The Civil Engineering Podcast which has been downloaded 1 million times, and author of the bestselling book for engineers entitled Engineer Your Own Success: 7 Key Elements to Creating an Extraordinary Engineering Career (by IEEE-Wiley Press). He is the founder of the Engineering Management Institute which has helped thousands of engineering professionals develop their management and leadership Live Webinar for Environmental Partners Group Engineers and Consultants Monday, October 21st at 12:30 pm EDT

Developing the Engineering Leader Inside of You by Engaging the People You Lead (1 PDH)

Engineering professionals are rarely taught management and leadership skills in school. They have to learn these skills on the job or suffer the consequences. One of the key components of leadership in engineering is PEOPLE. Great leaders know how to engage and inspire people. In this session, you will learn how to implement specific strategies that can help you maximize your relationships with the people you work with, like the power of positivity, the importance of open communication with your staff, understanding your role as a leader, and effective delegation. This session will also cover the theory of high output management. This session will:
  • Help you to realize that you are already a leader,
  • Discuss the power of positivity in leadership,
  • Provide strategies for seeing opportunities in every situation, specifically a two-question framework,
  • Give recommendations for utilizing leverage and delegating the right tasks, and
  • Discuss the importance of earning respect from your peers and how to best engage employees and maximize their potential.
About Your Speaker: Anthony Fasano, P.E. is a globally recognized best-selling author, speaker, host of The Civil Engineering Podcast which has been downloaded 1 million times, and author of the bestselling book for engineers entitled Engineer Your Own Success: 7 Key Elements to Creating an Extraordinary Engineering Career (by IEEE-Wiley Press). He is the founder of the Engineering Management Institute which has helped thousands of engineering professionals develop their management and leadership item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: anthonyfasano
item.content:encoded:
Updated: April 9, 2020 This Privacy Policy governs the manner in which the Engineering Management Institute collects, uses, maintains and discloses information collected from users (each, a “Student”) of the Engineering Management Institute. This Privacy Policy applies to the Engineering Management Institute (EMI) and all Products, Services, Courses and Content offered by the EMI. Personal identification information We may collect personal identification information from Students in a variety of ways, including, but not limited to, when Students enroll in the EMI or a Course within the EMI, subscribe to a newsletter, and in connection with other activities, services, features, or resources we make available through the EMI. Students may visit the EMI anonymously. We will collect personal identification information from Students only if they voluntarily submit such information to us. Students can refuse to supply personal identification information but doing so may prevent them from engaging in certain EMI related activities. How we use collected information EMI may collect and use Students’ personal identification information for the following purposes:
  • To improve customer service Information you provide helps us respond to your customer service requests and support needs more efficiently.
  • To personalize user experience We may use information in the aggregate to understand how our Students as a group use the services and resources provided in our School.
  • To send periodic emails We may use Student email addresses to send Students information and updates pertaining to their order. Student email addresses may also be used to respond to Student inquiries, questions, or other requests.
Sharing your personal information We do not sell, trade, or rent Student personal identification information to others. Third party websites Student may find advertising or other content on our EMI websites, content or courses that link to the websites and services of our partners, suppliers, advertisers, sponsors, licensors and other third parties. We do not control the content or links that appear on these websites and are not responsible for the practices employed by websites linked to or from EMI. In addition, these websites or services, including their content and links, may be constantly changing. These websites and services may have their own privacy policies and customer service policies. Browsing and interaction on any other website, including websites which have a link to our Student, is subject to that website's own terms and policies. Changes to this Privacy Policy EMI has the discretion to update this Privacy Policy at any time. We encourage Students to frequently check this page for any changes. You acknowledge and agree that it is your responsibility to review this Privacy Policy periodically and become aware of modifications. Your acceptance of these terms By consuming EMI’s content, signing up for our email list, subscribing to our social media channels or enrolling in our courses, you signify your acceptance of this Privacy Policy. If you do not agree to this Privacy Policy, please do not enroll in our programs. Your continued consumption, subscription or enrollment to EMI’s services following the posting of changes to this Privacy Policy will be deemed your acceptance of those changes.

item.title: TSEC Podcast Guest Speaker Form
item.dc:creator: AirizeVelasco
item.content:encoded:
Podcast Guest Information

Thank you for agreeing to come on The Structural Engineering Channel as a guest and share your wisdom with our listeners. Please fill out the form below and once you hit submit, you will be redirected to a scheduling page where you can select a time and date for the podcast interview that is convenient for you. By completing this form you agreeing to our podcast guest guidelines (click to read). Thanks again!

Thank you for agreeing to come on The Structural Engineering Channel as a guest and share your wisdom with our listeners. Please fill out the form below and once you hit submit, you will be redirected to a scheduling page where you can select a time and date for the podcast interview that is convenient for you. By completing this form you agreeing to our podcast guest guidelines (click to read). Thanks again!

Thank you for agreeing to come on The Structural Engineering Channel as a guest and share your wisdom with our listeners. Please fill out the form below and once you hit submit, you will be redirected to a scheduling page where you can select a time and date for the podcast interview that is convenient for you. By completing this form you agreeing to our podcast guest guidelines (click to read). Thanks again!

item.title: CEEP Guest Speaker Submission Form
item.dc:creator: AirizeVelasco
item.content:encoded:
Podcast Guest Information

Thank you for agreeing to come on the Civil Engineering Entrepreneurs Podcast as a guest and share your wisdom with our listeners. Please fill out the form below that will give us all of the information we need to prepare for the interview. By completing this form you agreeing to our podcast guest guidelines (click to read). Thanks again!

[gravityform id="155" title="true"]

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: If you enjoyed this post, please consider leaving a comment or subscribing to the RSS feed to have future articles delivered to your feed reader.
